mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce the discount rate for equity valuations, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC remains below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger immediate negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns remain positive in over 55% of instances where UNRATE rises by more than 0.2pp.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon through improved corporate revenue expectations.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns, with a regression coefficient on the interaction term statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production levels signal strengthening manufacturing output that translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns, but only during periods of stable inflation where CPIAUCSL growth remains below 2.5% annually.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 20-day returns when CPIAUCSL annual growth exceeds 2.5%, or the predictive power vanishes in high-inflation regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly increases in real gross domestic product reflect underlying economic growth that systematically generates positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth shows zero or negative correlation with 60-day forward returns across multiple business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation accelerates beyond a 3.0% annual threshold, equity valuations contract due to anticipated monetary tightening, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in more than 60% of months where CPIAUCSL annual growth surpasses 3.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market tightening, which triggers immediate positive S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE fails to predict positive 5-day returns, with out-of-sample accuracy below 52% over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in industrial production combined with falling retail sales indicate a supply-demand mismatch that depresses corporate earnings expectations, yielding negative S&P 500 returns over 60 days, specifically during late-cycle phases where FEDFUNDS exceeds 1.5%.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO and RSAFS trends do not predict negative 60-day returns when FEDFUNDS is above 1.5%, or the signal reverses direction in late-cycle environments.
